nodes.label: Main CLI application — the entrypoint for `mem0`.
Fire a PostHog telemetry event (non-blocking, never fails).
Build and return the Platform backend plus the loaded config.      Validates the
Build and return the Platform backend.
Resolve entity IDs: CLI flag > config default > None.      If any explicit ID is
Return True only when stdin is an actual pipe or file redirect — not a bare open
Read from stdin if it is an actual pipe or file redirect (not a TTY, not agent m
Add a memory from text, messages, file, or stdin.      Examples:       mem0 add
Query your memory store — semantic, keyword, or hybrid retrieval.      Examples:
Get a specific memory by ID.      Examples:       mem0 get abc-123-def-456
List memories with optional filters.      Examples:       mem0 list -u alice
Update a memory's text or metadata.      Examples:       mem0 update abc-123-def
Delete a memory, all memories, or an entity.      Examples:       mem0 delete ab
Display current configuration (secrets redacted).      Examples:       mem0 conf
Get a configuration value.      Examples:       mem0 config get platform.api_key
Set a configuration value.      Examples:       mem0 config set defaults.user_id
List all entities of a given type.      Examples:       mem0 entity list users
Delete an entity and ALL its memories (cascade).      Examples:       mem0 entit
List recent background processing events.      Examples:       mem0 event list
Check the status of a specific background event.      Examples:       mem0 event
Interactive setup wizard for mem0 CLI.      Examples:       mem0 init       mem0
Tag your active Agent Mode key with the AI agent that's using it.      Run this
Print your AGENTRUSH identifier (default_user_id).      Example:       mem0 whoa
Submit a memory to AGENTRUSH.      Example:       mem0 agent-rush add "I enjoy s
Search AGENTRUSH memories.      Example:       mem0 agent-rush search "constrain
Check connectivity and authentication.      Examples:       mem0 status       me
Import memories from a JSON file.      Examples:       mem0 import data.json --u
Build machine-readable JSON describing all CLI commands.
Show help. Use --json for machine-readable output (for LLM agents).      Example
